title: Reconcile canonical home of UserId/AgentId across Step 6 and Step 7
description:
## Problem
Beads bd-jdi (Step 6) and bd-3gm (Step 7) prescribe asymmetric strategies for the canonical home of the UUID newtypes:

- bd-jdi instructs: MOVE `UserId` out of `src/history.rs` into `src/user.rs` and re-export from `src/history.rs` via `pub use crate::user::UserId;` (canonical home becomes `user.rs`).
- bd-3gm instructs: keep `AgentId` canonical in `src/history.rs` and re-export from `src/agent.rs` via `pub use crate::history::AgentId;` (canonical home stays `history.rs`).

These two policies disagree on direction. If both are implemented as written, the codebase will have `history.rs` as the canonical home for `AgentId` but a re-exporter for `UserId`, which is gratuitous asymmetry. It will also make `Block::UserMessage { from: UserId }` resolve through a re-export path while `Block::AgentMessage { from: AgentId }` resolves directly - additional cognitive load for no benefit.

## Constraint
Both `UserId` and `AgentId` are referenced from `Block` variants in `history.rs`. Moving either definition out of `history.rs` creates a circular-looking dependency (`history` <- `user`/`agent`) that, while compilable, inverts the natural layering: low-level data types depending on higher-level domain modules.

## Resolution
Choose ONE policy and apply it to both types. Recommended:

- Keep `UserId` and `AgentId` canonically in `src/history.rs` (they are tightly coupled to `Block` variants).
- Re-export from `src/user.rs` and `src/agent.rs` so the public API surface offers `pristine::user::UserId` and `pristine::agent::AgentId`.

This requires editing bd-jdi to drop the MOVE instruction and replace it with `pub use crate::history::UserId;` - mirroring what bd-3gm already says for `AgentId`. No code changes to existing files are needed; this is a Bead-text fix.

## Acceptance
- bd-jdi description is amended so its `src/user.rs` instructions read: `pub use crate::history::UserId;` (no MOVE, no inversion of canonical home).
- bd-3gm description is unchanged.
- This Bead is closed with a one-line note pointing at the amended bd-jdi.

## Why P1
Step 6 and Step 7 will likely run in close succession or in parallel; landing them with conflicting policies will require a follow-up cleanup commit. Fixing the Bead text now is cheap.

title: Add live Anthropic smoke test for tool_use streaming parser
description:
The current tests/anthropic_live.rs smoke test exercises only text-only completions (System + User turns, asserts >=1 ContentDelta). With Phase 2 complete, the Anthropic adapter now parses tool_use streaming events (content_block_start with type=tool_use, content_block_delta with type=input_json_delta, content_block_stop) — handled at src/model/anthropic.rs:392-514 — but this path has ONLY synthetic unit-test coverage.

A regression in the live wire format (Anthropic changing field names like partial_json, renaming input_json_delta, altering the content_block_start envelope for tool_use) would NOT be caught by unit tests but WOULD be caught by a live smoke test. This is exactly what the live smoke test exists for.

## Scope

Add a second #[ignore]-gated #[tokio::test] in tests/anthropic_live.rs (or a sibling file) that:

1. Reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from env; skip if unset (mirrors existing live test).
2. Builds an AnthropicModel directly (does NOT need full Harness — the goal is to validate the adapter, not the agent loop).
3. Constructs a ModelInput with:
   - One Role::System turn (terse instruction to use the tool).
   - One Role::User turn (a prompt that requires arithmetic, e.g. 'What is 17 plus 25? Use the add tool.').
   - A ToolSpec describing an 'add' tool: name='add', description matches AddTool, input_schema={a:number, b:number}.
4. Drives model.complete(&input) and asserts that the stream yields at least:
   - one ModelStreamEvent::ToolUseStart with name=='add'
   - one ModelStreamEvent::ToolUseComplete with parseable JSON input containing fields a and b
5. Uses a 30s timeout consistent with the existing live test.

The test does NOT need to assert exact numeric values or that the model arrives at the answer — only that the tool_use wire format is correctly parsed end-to-end.

## Acceptance

- New live test is #[ignore]-gated so cargo nextest run still passes without the API key.
- Running it via cargo nextest run --run-ignored only with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set passes and detects ToolUseStart + ToolUseComplete from the live API.
- No changes to production code in src/.
- DoD: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy, cargo nextest run all pass (the new test is ignored so it does not affect default test run).

## Context

Filed by the Tidy Agent at end of Phase 2 (Tool Calls). All 16 Phase 2 beads closed, 90/90 tests passing. This is the single P1 finding from the end-of-phase entropy review — the live wire-format coverage gap is the highest-leverage gap before Phase 3 (Configuration File) begins.

title: Phase 2 Step 7a: Compile tool blocks and populate ModelInput.tools
description:
Replace the existing Block::ToolCall/Block::ToolResult skip in Agent::run with actual emission as ContentPart::ToolUse/ContentPart::ToolResult, and populate ModelInput.tools from the Agent's ToolRegistry. This is the DATA-FLOW side of the run-loop integration: it makes the Model aware of available tools and of prior tool exchanges in History. The CONTROL-FLOW side (dispatching tool calls returned by the model) is bd-7b.

After this bead, the Agent will pass a populated tools list to the Model and serialize prior ToolCall/ToolResult blocks into the model context, but if the model emits a ToolUseComplete event the existing no-op match arm continues to silently absorb it. No tool dispatch yet.

Scope -- src/agent.rs only:

1. Replace the History-to-ModelInput compilation in `Agent::run` (around lines 156-173). The existing code:
   ```rust
   for block in context {
       match block {
           Block::UserMessage { content, .. } => turns.push(Turn { role: Role::User, content: vec![ContentPart::Text(content)] }),
           Block::AgentMessage { content, .. } => turns.push(Turn { role: Role::Assistant, content: vec![ContentPart::Text(content)] }),
           Block::ReasoningTrace { .. }
           | Block::ToolCall { .. }
           | Block::ToolResult { .. } => {
               // Phase 1: these variants are kept in History but not routed to the model.
           }
       }
   }
   ```
   Refactor to:
   - Block::UserMessage -> Role::User Turn with one ContentPart::Text(content), as today.
   - Block::AgentMessage -> Role::Assistant Turn with one ContentPart::Text(content), as today.
   - Block::ToolCall { id, name, arguments, .. } -> Role::Assistant Turn with one ContentPart::ToolUse { id: id.clone(), name: name.clone(), input: arguments.clone() }.
   - Block::ToolResult { tool_use_id, result, is_error, .. } -> Role::User Turn with one ContentPart::ToolResult { tool_use_id: tool_use_id.clone(), content: result.clone(), is_error: *is_error }. (Note: Block::ToolResult.name is intentionally dropped here -- ContentPart::ToolResult doesn't carry a name; correlation is via tool_use_id.)
   - Block::ReasoningTrace stays skipped (no current way to round-trip into the Model -- ARMs vary). Single-line comment retained.

   Implementation hint: per-block emit ONE Turn. Even though Anthropic's protocol prefers consolidating consecutive same-role content blocks into one message, that consolidation is the provider adapter's responsibility. The Model trait sees one Turn per Block; the Anthropic adapter already handles content-block ordering correctly inside model_input_to_anthropic.

2. Populate ModelInput.tools from the Agent's ToolRegistry. Currently `Agent::run` builds `ModelInput { turns, tools: Vec::new() }`. Replace `Vec::new()` with a tools vector built from `self.tools.list()`:
   ```rust
   let tools: Vec<ToolSpec> = self.tools.list()
       .into_iter()
       .map(|tool| ToolSpec {
           name: tool.name().to_string(),
           description: tool.description().to_string(),
           input_schema: tool.input_schema().clone(),
       })
       .collect();
   ```
   Build this AFTER `let context = self.history.linearize();` but BEFORE the `let input = ModelInput { turns, tools };` so the snapshot is consistent within one iteration.

3. Required imports in src/agent.rs (add or reuse):
   - `ToolSpec` from `crate::model`.
   - `ContentPart` already in scope.

Tests in src/agent.rs `mod tests`:

Use the existing StubArModel (which already captures `last_input`). DO NOT modify StubArModel in this bead -- it currently supports single-call usage which is all this bead needs.

Add an inline `EchoTool` struct (mirror the pattern in bd-2i9's tests; modest duplication is acceptable).

Add these tests:

- `agent_run_populates_model_input_tools_from_registry` -- build an Agent with a registry containing one EchoTool. Send a UserMessage. Drain to RunComplete. Use `captured.last_input()` to fetch the ModelInput. Assert `input.tools.len() == 1`, `input.tools[0].name == "echo"`, and `input.tools[0].description == "<expected>"`. Use whatever description the EchoTool returns.

- `agent_run_compiles_block_tool_call_into_content_part_tool_use` -- pre-populate the Agent's History with a Block::ToolCall before sending a UserMessage. Since History is private to Agent, do this by sending sequenced inbound blocks: first a UserMessage, then drain to RunComplete; then send a SECOND UserMessage and confirm. Wait -- that won't work because Block::ToolCall isn't currently appended by external mechanisms.

  REVISED APPROACH: this test can construct History indirectly. Since the run loop appends blocks during streaming, the simplest path is to inject a ToolCall via a STUB whose script emits a stream containing nothing meaningful but BEFORE building the agent, manually pre-populate History via the AgentBuilder OR access `agent.history` directly.

  RULES OF THE BEAD: do NOT add a public History setter. Instead, exercise this path by sending a ToolCall Block via the MessageBus's `send_inbound` (which appends ANY Block to History). Then send a UserMessage afterward. The agent's compilation of context-into-ModelInput will include the ToolCall as an Assistant Turn with ContentPart::ToolUse.

  Concretely:
  - Send `Block::ToolCall { id: "use_x", name: "echo", arguments: serde_json::json!({"a":1}), timestamp: SystemTime::now() }` via `bus.send_inbound`.
  - Send a UserMessage via `bus.send_inbound`.
  - Drain to RunComplete.
  - From `captured.last_input()`, find the Assistant Turn whose content[0] is ContentPart::ToolUse with `id == "use_x"`, `name == "echo"`, `input == {"a":1}`.

- `agent_run_compiles_block_tool_result_into_content_part_tool_result` -- analogous, sending `Block::ToolResult { tool_use_id: "use_x", name: "echo", result: serde_json::json!("ok"), is_error: false, timestamp: SystemTime::now() }` and verifying it lands as a Role::User Turn whose content[0] is ContentPart::ToolResult with `tool_use_id == "use_x"`, `content == "ok"`, `is_error == false`.

Note on test mechanics: `bus.send_inbound` accepts any Block; the Agent unconditionally appends it. This is sufficient for setting up test History without making History mutable from outside. The model is called immediately, so we use a single-event StubArModel script.

Out of scope -- DO NOT DO:
- Dispatch tools when the model emits ToolUseComplete (bd-7b).
- Re-enter the model loop after a tool dispatch (bd-7b).
- Modify StubArModel (bd-7b will).
- Add a demo tool to the binary (bd-8).
- Update DESIGN.md or ARCHITECTURE.md.
- Add dependencies.

Constraints:
- No new dependencies.
- No `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.
- RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md: no banner comments, no decorative dividers, no usage examples in doc-comments.
- The existing no-op match arm in Agent::run for ToolUseStart/ToolUseDelta/ToolUseComplete remains untouched -- it is bd-7b's job to replace it.

Definition of Done:
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` (zero warnings)
- `cargo nextest run` (within 120s)

title: Phase 2 Step 5c: Anthropic adapter: parse tool_use streaming events
description:
Teach the Anthropic adapter's streaming response parser to recognize tool_use content blocks and emit the corresponding ModelStreamEvent variants (ToolUseStart, ToolUseDelta, ToolUseComplete). bd-29r landed the request-side serialization; this bead is response-side parsing only.

Anthropic SSE format reference (for the prompt; do NOT cite verbatim in code comments):

A tool_use content block produces three event kinds:
- `content_block_start` with payload `{"type":"content_block_start","index":<u32>,"content_block":{"type":"tool_use","id":"<id>","name":"<name>","input":{}}}` -- input is always an empty object here.
- `content_block_delta` with payload `{"type":"content_block_delta","index":<u32>,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"<string fragment>"}}` -- a JSON-text fragment for the tool's input. Multiple deltas per block.
- `content_block_stop` with payload `{"type":"content_block_stop","index":<u32>}` -- closes the block.

Existing parser state (in `impl ARModel for AnthropicModel::complete`, the spawned task) handles text blocks via a single `accumulator: String`. The new requirement is per-index state because Anthropic may interleave content blocks (text and tool_use) within one response. Anthropic's current model emits one content block at a time, but the protocol does not guarantee this -- being correct now avoids a flaky parser later.

Scope -- src/model/anthropic.rs only:

1. Replace the single `accumulator: String` with a per-index state map. Suggested shape:
   ```rust
   use std::collections::HashMap;

   enum BlockState {
       Text { accumulator: String },
       ToolUse { id: String, name: String, json_accumulator: String },
   }

   let mut blocks: HashMap<u32, BlockState> = HashMap::new();
   ```
   Place the type local to the function (or as a private module-level item if it cleans up the code). Keep the surface minimal.

2. Refactor `content_block_start` handling:
   - Deserialize a payload that captures `index` and a tagged inner `content_block`:
     ```rust
     #[derive(serde::Deserialize)]
     struct ContentBlockStartPayload {
         index: u32,
         content_block: ContentBlockStartInner,
     }

     #[derive(serde::Deserialize)]
     #[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
     enum ContentBlockStartInner {
         Text { #[serde(default)] text: String },
         ToolUse { id: String, name: String, #[serde(default)] input: serde_json::Value },
         #[serde(other)]
         Other,
     }
     ```
   - On `Text`: insert `BlockState::Text { accumulator: String::new() }` at the given index. (If the API ever sends a non-empty `text` here, prepend it to the accumulator -- defensive.)
   - On `ToolUse`: insert `BlockState::ToolUse { id, name, json_accumulator: String::new() }`. Emit `ModelStreamEvent::ToolUseStart { id: id.clone(), name: name.clone() }` to the channel.
   - On `Other`: insert nothing; do not emit. This keeps forward compatibility with future block types.

3. Refactor `content_block_delta` handling:
   - Deserialize a payload capturing `index` and the typed delta:
     ```rust
     #[derive(serde::Deserialize)]
     struct ContentBlockDeltaPayload {
         index: u32,
         delta: ContentDelta,
     }

     #[derive(serde::Deserialize)]
     #[serde(tag = "type")]
     enum ContentDelta {
         #[serde(rename = "text_delta")]
         TextDelta { text: String },
         #[serde(rename = "input_json_delta")]
         InputJsonDelta { partial_json: String },
         #[serde(other)]
         Other,
     }
     ```
   - On `TextDelta`: look up the index in `blocks`; if `Some(BlockState::Text { accumulator })`, push `text` and emit `ModelStreamEvent::ContentDelta { text }`. If the index is missing or mismatched, silently drop (defensive: a misordered/duplicated event should not crash the stream).
   - On `InputJsonDelta`: if `Some(BlockState::ToolUse { id, json_accumulator, .. })`, push `partial_json` and emit `ModelStreamEvent::ToolUseDelta { id: id.clone(), partial_json: partial_json.clone() }`. Missing index: silently drop.
   - On `Other`: silently drop.

4. Refactor `content_block_stop` handling:
   - Deserialize `{index: u32}`. (Add `ContentBlockStopPayload` if needed.)
   - Remove the entry at `index` from `blocks`. Match on the removed `BlockState`:
     - `Text { accumulator }`: emit `ModelStreamEvent::ContentComplete { text: accumulator }`.
     - `ToolUse { id, name, json_accumulator }`: parse `json_accumulator` as JSON via `serde_json::from_str`. If parsing succeeds, emit `ModelStreamEvent::ToolUseComplete { id, name, input }`. If parsing fails, send a single `Err(Error::Deserialization(format!("tool_use input parse: {e}")))` and return from the spawned task -- consistent with how the existing parser handles other deserialization failures.
     - If the index was not in the map (e.g. spurious stop), silently drop.

5. Preserve the existing `message_start`, `message_delta`, `message_stop` handling and the `Usage` reporting -- they are independent of the per-block state.

6. Delete the existing `accumulator: String` local and the `content_block_start` clearing of it.

7. The existing `ContentDelta` type at the top of the file (with `#[serde(tag = "type")]` and `TextDelta`/`Other` variants) is replaced by the more capable version above. The `ContentBlockDeltaPayload` struct is reused.

Tests -- add to `src/model/anthropic.rs` `mod tests`:

This bead's logic is hard to unit-test without an HTTP server, but the deserialization helpers can be exercised directly. Add:

- `parses_content_block_start_text` -- construct a JSON string matching the Anthropic text-block-start shape; deserialize into `ContentBlockStartPayload`; assert the variant is `Text` and the index is correct.
- `parses_content_block_start_tool_use` -- same with the tool_use shape; assert variant `ToolUse` with id/name fields.
- `parses_content_block_start_unknown_type_falls_back_to_other` -- a synthetic `{"type":"image","index":3,"content_block":{"type":"image"}}`; deserialize; assert `ContentBlockStartInner::Other`.
- `parses_input_json_delta` -- payload `{"type":"content_block_delta","index":1,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"{\"hello\":\"wo"}}`; deserialize as `ContentBlockDeltaPayload`; assert the delta variant matches `InputJsonDelta` with the expected partial_json string.
- `accumulated_tool_use_json_round_trips_via_serde_json` -- concatenate three partial_json fragments `{"text"`, `:"hi"`, `}`; pass through `serde_json::from_str`; assert the resulting `serde_json::Value` equals `json!({"text":"hi"})`.

Do NOT attempt to test the spawned-task / SSE flow end-to-end in this bead -- that requires either a mock HTTP server or an actual API call, both of which are out of scope here. The live anthropic test (`tests/anthropic_live.rs`) covers the integrated path when explicitly run with credentials.

Out of scope -- DO NOT DO:
- Touch src/model.rs, src/agent.rs, src/tool.rs, src/history.rs, src/harness.rs, src/messagebus.rs, src/stdio.rs, src/rpc.rs.
- Wire a ToolRegistry into Agent or Harness (separate bead).
- Update DESIGN.md, ARCHITECTURE.md.
- Add dependencies.

Constraints:
- No new dependencies.
- No `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.
- RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md: no banner comments, no decorative dividers, no usage examples in doc-comments.
- All errors from JSON parsing inside the spawned task must use the existing `Error::Deserialization` variant pattern.
- Be defensive: missing index, unknown content_block types, and unknown delta types must not crash the parser.

Definition of Done:
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` (zero warnings)
- `cargo nextest run` (within 120s)

Commit policy: one atomic commit. Do NOT modify .beads/issues.jsonl or .beads/last-touched. Use the trailer `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>`.

title: Phase 1 Step 8: Binary
description:
## Goal
Wire the `1p`/`pristine` binary to: parse CLI args with Clap; read `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` from env; construct a Harness with one `AnthropicModel` registered under `ModelRole::Default`; register one Agent with a hard-coded `system_prompt`; start the Harness; subscribe to its outbound event stream; send TWO sequential `UserMessage`s from the Owner; print `TokenDelta` text to stdout as it arrives; print usage on `RunComplete`; then `shutdown()` and `await` the Harness future cleanly.

The deliverable is the end-to-end demo from `ARCHITECTURE.md` §"Initial Build" and plan.md §"Step 8: Binary".

## Reference docs
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` §"Initial Build" (authoritative for the end-to-end shape)
- `blueprint/pristine-phase1-architecture/plan.md` §"Step 8: Binary", §"Data flow", §"CLI surface"
- `STYLE_GUIDE.md`

## Pre-conditions
By the time this Bead runs, `Cargo.toml` should declare exactly ONE `[[bin]]` target named `pristine` pointing at `src/bin/pristine.rs` (bd-23q resolves the duplicate-target warning by removing the `1p` `[[bin]]` entry). This Bead RE-INTRODUCES the `1p` alias as a separate thin shim binary (see §"`1p` shim" below) so the user's success criterion (`1p` binary) is met without re-introducing the duplicate-target warning.

## Implement in `src/bin/pristine.rs` (full replacement)
- Keep `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- Use `clap` derive.

### Clap surface
```
pristine run "first message"
```
Two-message demo. The first message is the positional CLI argument. The second message is hard-coded inside the binary as:

```
const SECOND_MESSAGE: &str = "Write me a poem of what it is like to be you, Pristine";
```

Per ARCHITECTURE.md §"Initial Build".

If no subcommand is given, default to `run`. Define one Clap subcommand `Run { message: String, #[arg(long, default_value = "claude-sonnet-4-6")] model: String }`. Document the `model` flag as the Anthropic model identifier.

### System prompt (hard-coded constant)
```
const SYSTEM_PROMPT: &str = "You are the Pristine agent. You have an identity that is uniquely yours!";
```

### Top-level error reporting
`#[tokio::main(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 2)]` and `async fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()>`. Convert crate errors with `?`. The `worker_threads = 2` floor is required to avoid deadlocks between the streaming HTTP body and the Agent loop.

### Stdout streaming loop
```
let mut rx = harness.subscribe(agent_id)?;
harness.send_to_agent(agent_id, owner_id, first_message)?;

let mut runs_completed = 0;
let mut second_sent = false;

while runs_completed < 2 {
    match rx.recv().await {
        Ok(AgentEvent::TokenDelta { text }) => {
            use std::io::Write;
            print!("{text}");
            std::io::stdout().flush()?;
        }
        Ok(AgentEvent::RunComplete { usage }) => {
            println!();
            eprintln!("[usage: input_tokens={} output_tokens={}]", usage.input_tokens, usage.output_tokens);
            runs_completed += 1;
            if !second_sent {
                harness.send_to_agent(agent_id, owner_id, SECOND_MESSAGE.into())?;
                second_sent = true;
            }
        }
        Ok(AgentEvent::BlockComplete { .. }) => {}
        Err(tokio::sync::broadcast::error::RecvError::Lagged(_)) => continue,
        Err(tokio::sync::broadcast::error::RecvError::Closed) => break,
    }
}

harness.shutdown();
harness.run_until_shutdown().await?;
Ok(())
```

### Env var handling
- Read `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` via `std::env::var`. If missing or empty, return `Err(anyhow::anyhow!("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set"))`.

### Construction
Use the Harness/Agent builders:
1. Build the `AnthropicModel` via `AnthropicModelBuilder::new().api_key(...).model_name(args.model.clone()).build()?`.
2. `HarnessBuilder::new().add_model(ModelId::new("anthropic-default"), Arc::new(model)).add_agent(PendingAgent { id: AgentId::new(), system_prompt: SYSTEM_PROMPT.into(), model_id: ModelId::new("anthropic-default") }).build()?`.
3. Capture the AgentId BEFORE building (so the binary knows what to subscribe to). Pattern: construct `let agent_id = AgentId::new();` first, pass it into `PendingAgent`, then call `build`.
4. `harness.start()?;`
5. Subscribe BEFORE sending: `let mut rx = harness.subscribe(agent_id)?;` then `harness.send_to_agent(agent_id, harness.owner_id(), args.message)?;`

## `1p` shim — implement in NEW file `src/bin/1p.rs`
```rust
#![forbid(unsafe_code)]
// Thin alias binary so users can invoke `1p`. The real implementation is in pristine::run.
fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
    pristine::run()
}
```

To support this, factor the body of `main` from `src/bin/pristine.rs` into a `pub fn run() -> anyhow::Result<()>` exported from the library at `src/lib.rs` (or via a new `src/bin_entry.rs` module). The factoring:
- Move the `#[tokio::main]`-wrapped logic into a synchronous wrapper that constructs the Tokio runtime explicitly:
  ```rust
  pub fn run() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
      let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread()
          .worker_threads(2)
          .enable_all()
          .build()?;
      rt.block_on(async { run_async().await })
  }
  async fn run_async() -> anyhow::Result<()> { /* the real logic */ }
  ```
- Both `src/bin/pristine.rs` and `src/bin/1p.rs` then have one-line `fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> { pristine::run() }` bodies.

Alternative simpler approach: keep `src/bin/pristine.rs` with `#[tokio::main]` and full logic; `src/bin/1p.rs` is one line: `fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> { pristine::run() }`. Implement `pub fn run()` in `lib.rs` as the runtime-bootstrapping wrapper described above. `src/bin/pristine.rs` should also call `pristine::run()` to avoid code duplication.

Recommendation: **the second approach** (both bins call `pristine::run()` from lib.rs; both bin files are one-line `fn main()`).

## Cargo.toml change
- Add a SECOND `[[bin]]` entry:
  ```
  [[bin]]
  name = "1p"
  path = "src/bin/1p.rs"
  ```
  Distinct files mean no duplicate-target warning.

The existing single `[[bin]]` entry (`pristine` → `src/bin/pristine.rs`, post bd-23q) is preserved.

## Tests
- No tests in this Bead. The behavior is interactive and live-API-dependent. End-to-end validation is the live `cargo run` invocation by the Coordinator after Judge passes.
- Optional but encouraged: a compile-only smoke test in `tests/binary_smoke.rs` (no API call) that invokes a CLAP `--help` rendering via `pristine::cli::parse_for_test(...)` — only if it fits naturally. NOT REQUIRED.

## Manual verification
After Judge passes, the Coordinator runs:
```
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=$(cat /home/nvader/projects/pristine/.env | grep ANTHROPIC_API_KEY | cut -d= -f2) \
  cargo run --bin 1p -- run "Introduce yourself to me, Pristine"
```
and confirms two sequential streaming responses print to stdout.

## Definition of Done
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo nextest run` — 25 prior tests pass + 1 ignored
- `cargo build --release` — succeeds
- `cargo build` — emits NO duplicate-target warning
- No `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`
- No STYLE_GUIDE.md violations

## Out of scope
- Configuration files
- Multi-agent demos
- Tool use

title: Phase 2 Step 10: Doc closeout - move Tool Calls to Completed and document architecture
description:
Final Phase 2 bead. Doc-only. Promote "Tool Calls" from DESIGN.md's Roadmap section into Completed, and add an ARCHITECTURE.md section describing the realised Tool subsystem (registry, trait, ToolSpec, run-loop cycle, Idle event, BlockComplete payload shape).

Scope -- DESIGN.md and ARCHITECTURE.md only. No source code changes.

### DESIGN.md edits

1. Add a new bullet to the `## Completed` section. Place it AFTER "JSON-RPC Server" so it preserves chronological order:

```markdown
**Tool Calls** -- Agents can invoke tools registered with the Harness. Defined the `Tool` trait and a `ToolRegistry`; extended the `ModelInput` contract with `tools: Vec<ToolSpec>` and `ContentPart::ToolUse`/`ToolResult`; extended `ModelStreamEvent` with streaming `ToolUseStart`/`Delta`/`Complete`; reconciled `Block::ToolCall`/`ToolResult` with correlation IDs; refactored the Anthropic adapter to serialize tools + tool content blocks and parse tool_use streaming events; wired the registry from `HarnessBuilder` through `Harness` to each `Agent`; replaced the History-to-ContentPart skip with real emission; added a tool-call cycle to `Agent::run` that dispatches tools and re-enters the model loop until the model returns no tool calls; surfaced an `AgentEvent::Idle` signal once per inbound block; expanded the JSON-RPC `block_complete` notification payload; shipped a built-in `add` tool. See ARCHITECTURE.md Tool Calls.
```

The wording is intentionally a single dense paragraph (the prior Completed bullets are also single paragraphs).

2. Remove the §Roadmap §1 "Tool Calls" entry entirely. Renumber the subsequent roadmap entries:

- "### 2. Configuration File" -> "### 1. Configuration File"
- "### 3. Multi-Model Support" -> "### 2. Multi-Model Support"
- "### 4. Skills" -> "### 3. Skills"
- "### 5. Multi-Agent Routing" -> "### 4. Multi-Agent Routing"
- "### 6. ContextCompiler" -> "### 5. ContextCompiler"
- "### 7. DLModel" -> "### 6. DLModel"

The body text of each entry remains unchanged (only the heading number changes).

If any internal cross-reference in DESIGN.md mentions a number (e.g. "§Roadmap.1"), update it to the new number where appropriate. Scan the file for "Roadmap" and "§1"-style references.

### ARCHITECTURE.md edits

Add a new top-level section titled `## Tool Calls` (or a sub-section under §Core Data Model Objects, your choice -- pick whichever respects the existing document layout most naturally).

The section content (markdown body) must cover:

1. **Tool trait** -- located in src/tool.rs. Three synchronous getters (`name`, `description`, `input_schema`) plus an async `call(input) -> Result<Value, ToolError>`. Implementors are `Send + Sync` and stored as `Arc<dyn Tool>`.

2. **ToolRegistry** -- name-keyed `HashMap<String, Arc<dyn Tool>>`. `register` rejects duplicate names; `dispatch(name, input)` returns either the tool's `Value` or a `ToolError::NotFound`/`InvalidInput`/`Execution`. The registry is owned by the Harness and shared (via `Arc`) with every Agent.

3. **ToolSpec on ModelInput** -- a provider-agnostic descriptor (`name`, `description`, `input_schema`) carried as `tools: Vec<ToolSpec>` alongside `turns`. ToolSpec carries no behavior; provider adapters translate it into their wire format. The Agent's run loop snapshots `self.tools.list()` into `ToolSpec`s per model call.

4. **ContentPart and Block extensions** -- `ContentPart::ToolUse { id, name, input }` and `ContentPart::ToolResult { tool_use_id, content, is_error }` carry tool exchanges at the Model boundary. `Block::ToolCall` and `Block::ToolResult` carry the matching correlation IDs (`id` and `tool_use_id`) so History → ContentPart compilation round-trips cleanly. `Block::ToolResult.name` is retained for human inspection but is dropped during ContentPart compilation (Anthropic correlates via id alone).

5. **The tool-call cycle** -- per inbound Block, `Agent::run` enters an inner loop:
   a. Build a `ModelInput { turns, tools }` snapshot from current History and registry.
   b. Stream the model. Accumulate any `ModelStreamEvent::ToolUseComplete` events into a pending list. Emit `AgentMessage` Block (only if text was produced) and `RunComplete`.
   c. If the pending list is empty, exit the inner loop.
   d. Otherwise dispatch each tool sequentially via the registry. Append `Block::ToolCall` then `Block::ToolResult` (with `is_error = false` on success or `is_error = true` carrying the rendered error). Loop back to (a).
   After the inner loop exits, emit `AgentEvent::Idle`.

6. **AgentEvent additions** -- `Idle` (no fields) fires once per outer inbound iteration. Subscribers use it as "agent ready for next input." `BlockComplete` notifications over JSON-RPC now carry a typed `data.block_type` discriminator -- one of `user_message`, `agent_message`, `tool_call`, `tool_result`, `reasoning_trace` -- with variant-appropriate fields.

7. **Error handling** -- a `ToolError` returned from dispatch does NOT propagate as `Agent::Error`. It is serialized as `{"error": "<message>"}` and stored in `Block::ToolResult { is_error: true, result, ... }`. The model sees the error on the next iteration and may recover.

8. **Anthropic adapter** -- the request body now uses typed content blocks (`text`, `tool_use`, `tool_result`) and emits a top-level `tools` array when `input.tools` is non-empty. The streaming parser recognizes `content_block_start` with type `tool_use` and `input_json_delta` content_block_delta events; it accumulates partial JSON per-index and emits `ToolUseStart`/`Delta`/`Complete` events.

9. **Built-in tools** -- `src/builtins.rs` ships an `AddTool` example. `pristine run` wires it into the `HarnessBuilder` so the binary demonstrates the loop end-to-end. Future built-ins live in the same module.

Style:
- Match the prose voice and section-header style used elsewhere in ARCHITECTURE.md (single-paragraph or two-paragraph sub-sections, no banner separators).
- Reference file paths inline as backticks (e.g. `src/tool.rs`).
- Do NOT include code snippets longer than 3 lines.
- Keep line wrap consistent with surrounding paragraphs (~80-100 cols).

Out of scope -- DO NOT DO:
- Touch any source file, Cargo manifest, justfile, gitignore, .beads/*, .claude/*, client.py.
- Add a code block beyond a short trait/method-signature reference.
- Reorganise the existing DESIGN.md or ARCHITECTURE.md sections beyond the changes above.

Constraints:
- No trailing whitespace.
- Heading hierarchy stays consistent (use `## Tool Calls` or `### Tools` as appropriate; do NOT introduce new top-level sections that break existing toc-style references).

Definition of Done:
- `git diff --check` reports no whitespace errors.
- `cargo nextest run` still passes (no source changes; sanity check).
- A spot check that the renumbered roadmap entries are coherent.

Commit policy: one atomic commit. Do NOT modify .beads/issues.jsonl or .beads/last-touched. Use the trailer `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>`.

title: Phase 2 Step 1: Tool trait and ToolRegistry
description:
Introduce a new src/tool.rs module defining the Tool trait and a ToolRegistry type. This is the foundational piece for the Tool Calls phase (see DESIGN.md Roadmap.1 and ARCHITECTURE.md History/Agent). This bead is integration-free: no Agent, Harness, or Model changes.

Scope:
- New file src/tool.rs. Register the module in src/lib.rs via `pub mod tool;`.
- Define a Tool trait with the following surface:
  - fn name(&self) -> &str
  - fn description(&self) -> &str
  - fn input_schema(&self) -> &serde_json::Value -- the JSON Schema describing the tool's input. Returned by reference; the implementor owns the schema.
  - An async call method that takes &self and input: serde_json::Value and returns Result<serde_json::Value, ToolError>. Use the boxed-future-return pattern that ARModel uses, OR re-use the async_trait re-exported through jsonrpsee::core::async_trait (no new dependency required). Pick whichever yields the cleaner ergonomics; document the choice with a one-line comment only if non-obvious. The trait must be Send + Sync + 'static capable so Arc<dyn Tool> can be stored.
- Define a ToolError enum with at least these variants: NotFound(String) (lookup by missing name), InvalidInput(String) (deserialization or schema violation), Execution(String) (errors raised by the tool body), AlreadyRegistered(String) (name collision during register). Implement Display and std::error::Error.
- Define a ToolRegistry struct that owns a HashMap<String, Arc<dyn Tool>>.
  - new() / Default
  - register(&mut self, tool: Arc<dyn Tool>) -> Result<(), ToolError>: inserts the tool keyed by tool.name().to_string(). On name collision, return ToolError::AlreadyRegistered; do not silently overwrite.
  - get(&self, name: &str) -> Option<Arc<dyn Tool>> for lookup.
  - list(&self) -> Vec<Arc<dyn Tool>> for adapters that need to emit a tool catalog (e.g. the Anthropic provider's tools array). Order is not specified; callers must not assume it.
  - dispatch(&self, name: &str, input: serde_json::Value) -> Result<serde_json::Value, ToolError> async convenience that does get then call.

Tests (in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` inside src/tool.rs):
- A stub tool that echoes its input. Test the full happy path: register, dispatch, observe echo result.
- Registering two tools with the same name returns ToolError::AlreadyRegistered.
- dispatch on an unknown name returns ToolError::NotFound.
- list returns all registered tools.
- ToolError implements std::error::Error (compile-time assertion via a generic function).

Constraints:
- No unwrap/expect in non-test code.
- No new dependencies in Cargo.toml.
- Follow RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md: no banner comments, no usage examples in doc-comments, no decorative comment dividers.
- Definition of Done (AGENTS.md): cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features (no warnings), cargo nextest run within 120s.

Out of scope (do NOT do in this bead):
- Wiring the registry into Harness or Agent.
- Touching Block, ContentPart, or ModelStreamEvent.
- Changing the Anthropic provider.
- Updating DESIGN.md or ARCHITECTURE.md.

Commit policy: one atomic commit that adds the file, registers the module, and adds the tests. The commit message should mention this is the foundation for the Tool Calls phase.

title: Reflection: forbid coding agents from running 'br close' on their own beads
description:
## Problem

During bd-1cs (first attempt, Judge-failed), the Coding Subagent ran `br close bd-1cs` itself before the Judge could review. This caused two cascading problems:

1. **DB / .jsonl divergence on retry.** `.beads/beads.db` is gitignored; `jj abandon @` does not roll back the DB. The retry attempt started against a 'closed' DB state and the .jsonl got sync'd back to closed by br's auto-flush.
2. **Bead-state commit pollution.** The close-state transition leaked into a later "Amend bd-1cs description" commit because of br's auto-flush boundary.

Subsequent beads mitigated this by emphatic 'do NOT run br close' instructions in the Coordinator's per-bead prompt. But this lives only in per-bead prompt construction — a new Coordinator instance has to rediscover the rule.

## Action

Edit `.claude/agents/coding.md` to add an explicit subsection (e.g. "## Bead state — do not touch") that states:

- The Coding Subagent MUST NOT run `br close`, `br update --status`, or any other state-mutating `br` command on its own bead.
- Only the Coordinator advances bead state, and only after the Judge passes.
- If the bead description asks the agent to update bead state, the agent should refuse and report the conflict to the Coordinator.

Optionally add a one-line reminder in the Definition of Done section of `.claude/agents/coding.md`: "Do not run `br close` — the Coordinator does that."

## Acceptance

- `.claude/agents/coding.md` updated.
- Single commit with the standard trailer.
- No code changes.

title: Resolve placeholder `pub enum Error {}` in src/lib.rs
description:
src/lib.rs:10 declares `pub enum Error {}` — an empty, uninhabited public enum with no constructors, no `Display`/`Error` impls, and no callers. It is dead surface area.

Two acceptable resolutions:
1. Remove the line entirely. Crate-level error consolidation can be designed when (or if) it is actually needed; right now each module that needs an Error type defines its own (e.g. `crate::model::Error`).
2. Replace it with a real top-level Error enum that wraps the module-level errors (e.g. `Model(crate::model::Error)`), with `From` impls and `Display` / `std::error::Error` implementations.

Recommended: option 1. The cost of premature consolidation is higher than the cost of adding it later. Steps 4-8 will reveal the actual shape needed.

Acceptance: src/lib.rs is clean of dead types. cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo nextest run all pass.

title: Phase 2 Step 4: Add correlation IDs to Block::ToolCall and Block::ToolResult
description:
Reconcile Block (History event vocabulary) with ContentPart (Model input vocabulary) so that tool exchanges carry the same `tool_use_id` correlation key end-to-end. The new ContentPart::ToolUse carries an `id` and ContentPart::ToolResult carries a `tool_use_id`; the corresponding Block variants currently do not. The integration beads cannot wire tool calls cleanly until this is fixed.

Scope -- src/history.rs only (with cascade fix-ups elsewhere):

Replace the existing Block::ToolCall and Block::ToolResult variants:

```rust
ToolCall {
    id: String,
    name: String,
    arguments: serde_json::Value,
    timestamp: SystemTime,
},
ToolResult {
    tool_use_id: String,
    name: String,
    result: serde_json::Value,
    is_error: bool,
    timestamp: SystemTime,
},
```

Field rationale:
- `Block::ToolCall.id`: provider-issued tool-use identifier. Must equal the eventual `ContentPart::ToolUse.id` emitted to the Model in subsequent re-runs.
- `Block::ToolResult.tool_use_id`: correlation key matching the originating `Block::ToolCall.id`. Will become `ContentPart::ToolResult.tool_use_id` when re-compiled.
- `Block::ToolResult.is_error`: success/failure flag. Mirrors `ContentPart::ToolResult.is_error`. Default semantics: false on success, true when the registry returned a `ToolError` or the tool's call body returned `Err`.
- `Block::ToolResult.name` is RETAINED. ContentPart::ToolResult does not carry a name (Anthropic protocol doesn't need it because correlation is via id), but History benefits from it for human inspection / debugging. The Agent simply drops the name when compiling to ContentPart.

Cascade fix-ups -- the new field shape WILL force exhaustive `match` updates:
- src/agent.rs production match (around line 155-170) that already destructures `Block::ReasoningTrace { .. } | Block::ToolCall { .. } | Block::ToolResult { .. } => { ... }` continues to use `{ .. }` so this compiles unchanged. Verify by build.
- src/messagebus.rs route() (around line 152-168) destructures `Block::AgentMessage { .. }` only -- no impact.
- src/history.rs tests do NOT currently construct ToolCall/ToolResult variants -- no impact expected.
- src/test_support.rs and tests/anthropic_live.rs: scan for any uses of Block::ToolCall/ToolResult; none expected, but verify.
- If clippy/rustc flags any other exhaustive-match site, update with minimum changes (zero logic changes).

Tests to add (inside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in src/history.rs):
- A test that appends a Block::ToolCall to a History and confirms via pattern-match that all four fields (id, name, arguments, timestamp) round-trip via linearize().
- A test that appends a Block::ToolResult (with `is_error: true`) and confirms all five fields round-trip via linearize().
- A test that appends a paired (ToolCall, ToolResult) sequence sharing the same id/tool_use_id and confirms the correlation key survives linearize().

Out of scope -- DO NOT DO:
- Touch src/agent.rs production logic beyond what is mechanically required for compilation. The existing skip arm for tool blocks remains in place.
- Wire Block::ToolCall/Block::ToolResult into Agent's History-to-ContentPart compilation (separate bead).
- Touch src/model.rs, src/tool.rs, src/harness.rs, src/messagebus.rs, src/stdio.rs, src/rpc.rs (except minimum no-op match arms forced by exhaustiveness checks).
- Update DESIGN.md, ARCHITECTURE.md.
- Add dependencies.

Constraints:
- No new dependencies.
- No `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.
- RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md: no banner comments, no decorative dividers, no usage examples in doc-comments.
- Named-field (struct-style) variants.

Definition of Done:
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` (zero warnings)
- `cargo nextest run` (within 120s)

Commit policy: one atomic commit. Do NOT modify .beads/issues.jsonl or .beads/last-touched. Use the trailer `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>`.

The trait impl holds Arc to shared Harness state (bus, agent_id, owner_id, shutdown CancellationToken). send_message calls bus.send_inbound and spawns a task that subscribes to agent events and forwards them as JSON-RPC notifications via jsonrpsee subscription sink. title: Unify agent.rs test drain idioms behind drain_until_idle helper
description:
Tidy/Reflection finding from end of Phase 2. The agent.rs test module has accreted three distinct drain idioms over the phase:

1. Inline 'idle_seen' boolean flag in 'agent_run_processes_one_user_message' (line ~386).
2. Inline 'idles == N' counter in 'agent_run_compiles_block_tool_call_into_content_part_tool_use' (line ~726) and 'agent_run_compiles_block_tool_result_into_content_part_tool_result' (line ~799).
3. Shared 'drain_until_idle' helper used in the three bd-1um tests (line ~843, called at ~926, ~1041, ~1135).

The helper exists but is only applied uniformly to the bd-1um tests. The earlier patterns were each added when a new bead required them and were never folded back. As Phase 3 adds new agent test patterns, this will compound.

## Scope

1. Generalize the helper into 'drain_until_idles(outbound, sink, n)' that accumulates events until the Nth Idle is observed. Document with a one-line doc-comment.
2. Replace all three idiom variants in src/agent.rs tests with calls to the unified helper.
3. Preserve every test's existing assertion semantics. The events vector each test inspects MUST contain the same events as before (events before the terminating Idle).
4. Do NOT change any non-test code in src/agent.rs.

## Acceptance

- Only one drain idiom remains in src/agent.rs tests.
- cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features, cargo nextest run all pass.
- Test count unchanged.
- No new dependencies.

## Context

Filed by the Reflection Agent at end of Phase 2 (Tool Calls). The prior Tidy passes (3 of them) examined documentation drift and fixture duplication but did not look at test-helper proliferation. This bead closes that gap before Phase 3 (Configuration File) adds new test patterns.

title: Phase 2 Step 5a: Add ToolSpec type and tools field to ModelInput
description:
Introduce a ToolSpec type in src/model.rs and extend ModelInput with a `tools: Vec<ToolSpec>` field. This is the metadata channel by which the Agent communicates the available tools to the Model per call. The Anthropic adapter will serialize this into the request body in the next bead.

Architectural rationale:
- ModelInput is the stable contract between Agent (or ContextCompiler) and Model. Provider request schemas (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini) bundle tool definitions alongside messages -- representing tools as a field of ModelInput keeps the trait surface uniform across providers without polluting Turn/ContentPart.
- ToolSpec is a small, provider-agnostic descriptor (`name`, `description`, `input_schema`). It is NOT `dyn Tool` -- it carries no behavior, only the JSON-schemaable shape needed to advertise the tool to the model. Adapters use ToolSpec to build provider-native request payloads; the Agent's ToolRegistry remains the source of truth for invocation.

Scope -- src/model.rs only (with cascade fix-ups, none expected):

1. Add `pub struct ToolSpec` with:
   ```rust
   pub struct ToolSpec {
       pub name: String,
       pub description: String,
       pub input_schema: serde_json::Value,
   }
   ```
   Derive `Clone, Debug`. No `Serialize`/`Deserialize` -- providers will translate ToolSpec into their own payload types.

2. Add `pub tools: Vec<ToolSpec>` field to the existing `pub struct ModelInput`.
   - Default to empty when the Agent has no tools (callers populate as needed).
   - Update the existing in-file constructor literal in the `mod tests` block (`ModelInput { turns: vec![...] }`) to also set `tools: Vec::new()` where required. Same for any literal usages elsewhere.

Cascade fix-ups -- forced by `tools` field addition:
- src/agent.rs Agent::run constructs `ModelInput { turns }` (around line 174). Update to `ModelInput { turns, tools: Vec::new() }` since the registry is not yet wired (separate bead). Single mechanical change.
- src/model/anthropic.rs serializes ModelInput. The current `model_input_to_anthropic` does not name `tools`. Build should still compile because the function only reads `input.turns`, but the empty `tools` field is implicitly threaded through. Confirm by build.
- src/test_support.rs StubArModel::last_input captures the input -- field-level destructuring should still work because `ModelInput` is `Clone`.
- Tests in src/model.rs, src/agent.rs, src/model/anthropic.rs, src/test_support.rs, tests/anthropic_live.rs may construct `ModelInput { turns: ... }` literally; add `tools: Vec::new()`.

Tests (inside src/model.rs `mod tests`):
- Construct a `ToolSpec` literal with all three fields populated; pattern-match to confirm field access.
- Construct a `ModelInput` with a non-empty `tools` vector (one ToolSpec); assert `input.tools.len() == 1` and the inner fields round-trip.
- Confirm `ToolSpec: Send + Sync` via the existing `assert_send_sync` helper.

Out of scope -- DO NOT DO:
- Wire the Agent to populate `tools` from a ToolRegistry (separate bead).
- Wire the Anthropic adapter to serialize `tools` into the request (separate bead bd-5b).
- Add provider-native types (e.g. AnthropicTool) in src/model/anthropic.rs (separate bead bd-5b).
- Touch src/history.rs, src/tool.rs, src/harness.rs, src/messagebus.rs, src/stdio.rs, src/rpc.rs.
- Update DESIGN.md, ARCHITECTURE.md.
- Add dependencies.

Constraints:
- No new dependencies.
- No `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.
- RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md: no banner comments, no decorative dividers, no usage examples in doc-comments.
- ToolSpec field `input_schema` must NOT be `&serde_json::Value` -- ToolSpec owns its data so it can be cloned freely. (The Tool trait returns `&serde_json::Value`; the Agent or adapter clones into a ToolSpec when assembling the request.)

Definition of Done:

title: Phase 2 Step 2: Extend ContentPart with ToolUse and ToolResult
description:
Extend the ModelInput contract so that tool use can be represented at the Model boundary. The ContentPart enum in src/model.rs is already marked `#[non_exhaustive]` precisely to accommodate this addition (see the comment above the enum). No Agent/Harness/Anthropic-adapter integration yet -- that lands in later beads. This bead is pure type-level extension plus tests.

Scope:
- In src/model.rs, extend `pub enum ContentPart` with two new variants:
  - `ToolUse { id: String, name: String, input: serde_json::Value }`
    - `id`: provider-issued opaque identifier for the tool-use request (used to correlate with its result).
    - `name`: tool name as registered in the ToolRegistry.
    - `input`: arguments passed to the tool (JSON value matching the tool's input_schema).
  - `ToolResult { tool_use_id: String, content: serde_json::Value, is_error: bool }`
    - `tool_use_id`: the `id` of the originating `ToolUse` part.
    - `content`: the JSON payload returned by the tool (or the error description if `is_error` is true).
    - `is_error`: true when the tool failed; false on success. Mirrors Anthropic's tool_result block shape and is the simplest representation that survives across providers.

- Update the existing comment above `ContentPart` to no longer say "future phases will add `ToolUse` and `ToolResult` variants" -- this bead implements that. Replace the comment with a brief, present-tense note explaining that the enum carries text and tool exchanges; keep `#[non_exhaustive]` because more variants (image, audio, etc.) are still anticipated.

- Add tests inside the existing `mod tests` block in src/model.rs:
  - Construct a `ModelInput` containing a `Turn` whose content holds a `ContentPart::ToolUse` and verify field access by pattern-match (basic smoke check).
  - Same for `ContentPart::ToolResult`.
  - Verify `ContentPart::ToolUse` and `ContentPart::ToolResult` are `Send + Sync` (use the existing `assert_send_sync` helper).
  - Confirm `Clone` on `ContentPart::ToolResult` round-trips fields (use `is_error: true` and a JSON null content to keep it minimal).

Out of scope -- DO NOT DO any of the following in this bead:
- Touch src/agent.rs (do NOT change the History-to-ModelInput compilation; the existing arms that skip ToolCall/ToolResult Blocks remain in place. A later bead will replace that skip).
- Touch src/model/anthropic.rs (do NOT serialize the new variants; the existing `model_input_to_anthropic` will simply ignore them via the non-exhaustive match wildcard if needed -- but adding a new wildcard arm that explicitly ignores ToolUse/ToolResult is a minimum-change extension and is acceptable. If the compiler does NOT force a wildcard because the function only matches role-level structure, do not add one.)
- Touch src/history.rs (Block::ToolCall does not gain an id field in this bead -- that is a separate bead).
- Add new ModelStreamEvent variants -- that is a separate bead.
- Touch DESIGN.md or ARCHITECTURE.md.
- Add dependencies.

Constraints:
- No new dependencies in Cargo.toml.
- No `unwrap`/`expect` in non-test code.
- RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md: no banner comments, no decorative dividers, no usage examples in doc-comments, comments concise and professional.
- The two new variants must use struct-style (named) fields for forward compatibility, matching the style of Block::ToolCall and Block::ToolResult in src/history.rs.

Definition of Done (verify before reporting success):
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` (no warnings)
- `cargo nextest run` (within 120s)

If the Anthropic adapter's match on ContentPart now produces a non-exhaustive-match warning because new variants exist, add a minimal explicit arm in `model_input_to_anthropic` that maps ToolUse/ToolResult to a no-op (push nothing to the messages text accumulator). Document with a single-line comment that the real handling lands in a later bead. This is an EXCEPTION to the "out of scope" rule for src/model/anthropic.rs and is permitted only if required to silence the warning.

Commit policy: one atomic commit. Do NOT modify .beads/issues.jsonl -- the Coordinator handles bead-state commits separately. Use the trailer `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>`.

title: Phase 1 Step 7: Agent event loop
Reflection: capture Coordinator's bead-state-split recipe as a documented script
description:
## Problem

Each closed bead currently costs the Coordinator a ~10-step mechanical recipe split across two commits: bead-state checkpoint, then the actual content commit, with `br close`, `cp`, `jj restore`, `jj new`, `cp`, `jj desc` interleaved. Across ~25 beads in the Built-in Tools inter-phase this is roughly 250 mechanical steps consuming Coordinator context.

The mechanics are correct — they keep `.beads/issues.jsonl` state changes in their own atomic commit, separate from the content. But the recipe is undocumented (it lives only in the Coordinator's running context) and re-derived per bead.

## Action

Add a section to `.claude/agents/coordinator.md` named something like "## Bead close recipe" that:

1. Documents the canonical recipe step-by-step (the exact sequence of `br close`, `cp`, `jj restore -r @-`, `jj new`, `cp`, `jj desc`, `jj new` calls — or whatever the current sequence is).
2. Names the two commits the recipe produces: `Close <id>: <title>` (content) and `Close <id> bead state` (jsonl-only).
3. Notes the rationale: `.beads/beads.db` is gitignored so only `.beads/issues.jsonl` and `.beads/last-touched` need to be in the bead-state commit.

The recipe does NOT need to be a script (the user has not asked for scripting). Documenting it in the agent doc is enough — it stops re-derivation and lets the next Coordinator copy-paste.

## Acceptance

- `.claude/agents/coordinator.md` has a clear, copy-pasteable recipe.
- Single commit, standard trailer.
- No code changes.

title: Built-in tools P-1: ToolError::Execution(serde_json::Value) evolution
description:
## Context

Prelude bead 1/4 of the Built-in Tools inter-phase (plan at blueprint/builtin-tools/PLAN.md, refined prompt at blueprint/builtin-tools/PROMPT.md). Lays the structural foundation: every built-in tool will surface errors as JSON Values, not strings, so per-tool typed error enums can serialize through one portable carrier (the "Portable shape, adapter dialect" principle in ARCHITECTURE.md).

## Required reading before coding

1. blueprint/builtin-tools/PLAN.md (sections "Decisions", "Type definitions", "ToolError::Execution(serde_json::Value) evolution", "P-1")
2. blueprint/builtin-tools/PROMPT.md (the "Error model" bullet)
3. ARCHITECTURE.md (the "Portable shape, adapter dialect" principle at the top)
4. src/tool.rs (current ToolError definition + Display impl)
5. src/builtins.rs (current AddTool::call implementation; uses ToolError::Execution(String))
6. src/lib.rs (search for "ToolError::Execution" and the Agent::run tool-dispatch arm)
7. src/agent.rs (the tool-call dispatch loop -- look for ToolError handling)

## Goal

Change `ToolError::Execution(String)` to `ToolError::Execution(serde_json::Value)`. All call sites compile. All existing tests continue to pass.

## Required changes

1. In src/tool.rs:
   - Change the `Execution` variant to carry `serde_json::Value`.
   - Update the `Display` impl so the Execution arm renders the Value via `serde_json::to_string(...)` (fall back to a literal `{}` JSON if to_string fails -- it cannot fail for a well-formed Value, but be defensive without unwrap).

2. In src/agent.rs (or wherever the tool-dispatch loop wraps a ToolError into a Value for the ToolResult content):
   - In the `Err(err)` arm of the tool-call dispatch, forward the Value verbatim when the variant is `ToolError::Execution(v)`. For other variants, wrap as `serde_json::json!({"error": <other.to_string()>})`. Pattern:
     ```rust
     let value = match err {
         ToolError::Execution(v) => v,
         other => serde_json::json!({"error": other.to_string()}),
     };
     ```

3. In src/builtins.rs (AddTool::call):
   - Where it currently returns `Err(ToolError::Execution("...".into()))`, replace the string with `serde_json::json!({"error": "<same message>"})`. This is the minimal cascade; the typed `AddError` enum lands in P-2, not here.

4. Any other call site that constructs `ToolError::Execution(...)` must be updated similarly. Use `rg "ToolError::Execution"` to find them.

## Out of scope

- Introducing `AddError` -- that is P-2.
- Touching any other tool or creating new submodules -- those are later beads.
- Changing the public signature of `Tool::call` (it already returns `Result<Value, ToolError>` -- only the contents of the Execution variant change).

## Definition of Done

- `cargo fmt --check` passes
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` passes (no warnings)
- `cargo nextest run` passes within 120s
- The diff is small and additive in style; no drive-by refactors or formatting sweeps
- One atomic commit with the project's standard trailer

## Plan-respect note

This bead is foundational for "Portable shape, adapter dialect": the Value carrier is the portable shape; per-tool typed enums (added in later beads) are the adapter dialect.

title: Add 'Design principles' section to ARCHITECTURE.md (Portable shape, adapter dialect)
description:
Inter-phase doc bead. Capture the foundational design principle Pristine has been walking since Phase 1: a portable layer that carries provider-neutral information, and adapters at the periphery that own provider-specific dialect.

This principle is referenced in the upcoming inter-phase work (Read/Write/Edit/Insert/ExecBash) -- making it explicit before the next coding subagent reads ARCHITECTURE.md.

Scope -- ARCHITECTURE.md only. No source code changes.

Insert a new top-level section titled `## Design Principles` between the document's opening text and the existing `## Goals` section. Section body:

```markdown
## Design Principles

### Portable shape, adapter dialect

Pristine's portable layer -- the engine, traits, and types that agents
and clients touch -- carries only the information that every targeted
provider, model, or client requires. Provider dialect (wire formats,
naming conventions, optional fields, error vocabularies, advertisement
mechanisms) lives in adapters at the periphery. The portable layer
never grows speculatively; a capability is promoted from
"adapter-specific" into the portable layer only when it surfaces as a
common pattern across multiple providers.

The principle has four consequences:

1. **Information vs. dialect.** Portable types capture *what* a turn,
   tool, or stream event is. Adapters capture *how* one provider
   encodes it on the wire. The split is load-bearing.
2. **No leaks downward.** When a feature is unique to one provider,
   the rest of the engine never learns about it. Substituting providers
   does not propagate changes through the core.
3. **Additive evolution.** The portable layer grows when a pattern is
   shared, not when one provider gains a feature. Promotion happens
   with at least two cross-provider examples in view -- never on
   speculation about one.
4. **Separation of concerns at every boundary.** System prompts carry
   identity and behavior. Tools carry behavior and self-description.
   Adapters carry transport and dialect. Clients carry presentation.
   Each layer owns what it owns and nothing else.

The Goals listed below -- composability, multi-agent isolation,
transport neutrality -- are consequences of this principle, not peer
principles.
```

Constraints:
- Doc-only. NO source code changes. NO Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock changes. NO changes to any file other than ARCHITECTURE.md.
- Section goes ABOVE `## Goals`, AFTER the title.
- Line wrap consistent with the rest of ARCHITECTURE.md (paragraphs at ~80 columns or whatever the surrounding text uses -- match existing).
- No trailing whitespace.
- Heading levels: `## Design Principles` is a top-level section; the principle name is `### Portable shape, adapter dialect`. No deeper headers.

Definition of Done:
- `git diff --check` clean (no whitespace errors).
- `cargo nextest run` passes (sanity check; no source changes, but confirm).
- The new section renders coherently: a reader landing on ARCHITECTURE.md sees the principle BEFORE the Goals section.

Commit policy: one atomic commit. Do NOT modify .beads/issues.jsonl or .beads/last-touched. Use trailer `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>`.

title: Edit T-3: unit tests covering every typed error + edge case
description:
## Context

Third Edit slice. Adds unit-test coverage for `Edit::call` exercising every typed error variant + every documented edge case.

## Required reading

1. blueprint/builtin-tools/PLAN.md (sections "Edit" under behavioral spec; "Testing strategy")
2. blueprint/builtin-tools/PROMPT.md (the "Edit" bullet)
3. src/builtins/edit.rs (the impl just landed in bd-ctk)
4. src/builtins/exec_bash.rs (template for `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block, especially for tests that need a temp working dir / filesystem fixture)
5. RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md (test code may use unwrap/expect freely)

## Goal

Add a `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block to `src/builtins/edit.rs` with comprehensive coverage. Test count should increase by at least the 9 distinct scenarios listed below. No changes to production code.

## Required test scenarios

Each is one `#[tokio::test]` (use the same `#[tokio::test]` style that ExecBash tests use):

1. **`edit_replaces_single_match_writes_file_atomically`**: Fixture has content `"foo bar baz"`. Call Edit with `{path, old_str: "bar", new_str: "BAR"}`. Assert:
   - `Ok(value)` with `value["replaced"] == true`.
   - File on disk now reads `"foo BAR baz"`.

2. **`edit_returns_multiple_matches_on_count_ge_2`**: Fixture has content `"foo foo foo"`. Call Edit with `old_str: "foo", new_str: "FOO"`. Assert:
   - `Err(ToolError::Execution(value))`.
   - `value["kind"] == "multiple_matches"`.
   - `value["count"] == 3`.
   - File on disk is UNCHANGED.

3. **`edit_returns_no_matches_on_zero`**: Fixture has content `"hello"`. Call Edit with `old_str: "xyz", new_str: "..."`. Assert:
   - `value["kind"] == "no_matches"`.
   - File unchanged.

4. **`edit_returns_no_matches_on_empty_old_str`**: Fixture has content `"hello"`. Call Edit with `old_str: "", new_str: "x"`. Assert:
   - `value["kind"] == "no_matches"` (per the documented decision).
   - File unchanged.

5. **`edit_returns_file_not_found_on_missing_path`**: Use a path that definitely does not exist (e.g., `tmpdir/nonexistent-{uuid}.txt`). Assert:
   - `value["kind"] == "file_not_found"`.
   - `value["path"]` matches the resolved path.

6. **`edit_returns_not_utf8_for_binary_file`**: Write a fixture file containing bytes `[0x48, 0x69, 0xFF, 0x80]` (Hi + invalid UTF-8). Call Edit with `old_str: "anything", new_str: "x"`. Assert:
   - `value["kind"] == "not_utf8"`.
   - `value["byte_offset"] == 2` (the position of the first invalid byte).

7. **`edit_returns_invalid_path_on_empty_string`**: Call Edit with `path: ""`. Assert:
   - `value["kind"] == "invalid_path"`.
   - `value["reason"]` is a non-empty string.

8. **`edit_old_eq_new_is_noop_success`**: Fixture has content `"hello"`. Call Edit with `old_str: "hello", new_str: "hello"`. Assert:
   - `Ok` with `value["replaced"] == true`.
   - File on disk unchanged (mtime may differ but content is identical -- you only need to assert content).

9. **`edit_preserves_trailing_newline`**: Fixture has content `"hello\n"`. Call Edit with `old_str: "hello", new_str: "HELLO"`. Assert:
   - `Ok({replaced: true})`.
   - File on disk now reads `"HELLO\n"` (newline preserved).

10. (Optional) **`edit_returns_invalid_input_on_malformed_json`**: Call `tool.call(json!({"path": "x"}))` (missing old_str/new_str). Assert `Err(ToolError::InvalidInput(_))`.

## Test fixtures

Use a per-test unique directory under `std::env::temp_dir()`. Generate uniqueness via `Uuid::new_v4().simple().to_string()` (uuid crate is in deps; same pattern ExecBash uses). Create the dir at the start of each test; do not bother cleaning up at the end (the OS reclaims temp dirs eventually).

Example helper inside the tests module:

```rust
fn unique_tempdir() -> std::path::PathBuf {
    use uuid::Uuid;
    let id = Uuid::new_v4().simple().to_string();
    let dir = std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("pristine-edit-test-{id}"));
    std::fs::create_dir_all(&dir).expect("create temp dir");
    dir
}
```

Use `std::fs::write` synchronously for fixture setup. The tool itself uses `tokio::fs`, so tests run as `#[tokio::test]`.

Tests freely use `unwrap()`/`expect()` (RUST_STYLE_GUIDE allows in test code).

## Out of scope

- Registration (T-4).
- ARCHITECTURE.md updates (T-4).
- Tests for OTHER tools (each tool gets its own T-3).
- Integration / smoke tests (no live Edit smoke test required by the plan — Edit doesn't spawn anything).

## Definition of Done

- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo nextest run` -- test count INCREASES by at least 9 from the baseline of 104 passed, 6 skipped. New count: 113+ passed, 6 skipped.
- `rg "\.unwrap\(\)|\.expect\(" src/builtins/edit.rs` may now return hits — but every hit must be inside the `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block.
- `jj diff --summary` shows exactly `M src/builtins/edit.rs`. No other files.
- Commit subject `Close bd-XXX: Edit T-3 (tests)` with the trailer.

## Plan-respect note

Tests verify the dialect (per-tool typed errors) without coupling to anything portable. Each variant of `EditError` gets its own assertion target — the dialect is fully exercised.

title: ExecBash T-4: register in HarnessBuilder + fill ARCHITECTURE.md ExecBash stub
description:
## Context

Fourth and final ExecBash slice. Wires the `ExecBash` tool into the harness's default registration chain and replaces the ARCHITECTURE.md placeholder stub with the real per-tool paragraph.

After this bead, `pristine run` registers BOTH the existing `AddTool` AND the new `ExecBash` tool, so an agent invoked via the harness can actually exec shell commands.

## Required reading

1. blueprint/builtin-tools/PLAN.md (sections "T-4. Registration"; "ExecBash" sub-section under "Per-tool behavioral specifications")
2. blueprint/builtin-tools/PROMPT.md (the "ExecBash" bullet)
3. src/lib.rs — find `run_async` (or wherever `HarnessBuilder` is constructed and tools are added). Look for the existing `add_tool(Arc::new(AddTool::new())?)` line — ExecBash will follow the same pattern.
4. ARCHITECTURE.md — locate the `### ExecBash` stub in the `## Built-in Tools` section (added by bd-76o, doc-prep).
5. src/builtins/exec_bash.rs — the actual ExecBash impl, to write an accurate paragraph.

## Goal

1. Register `ExecBash` in `HarnessBuilder` via `.add_tool(Arc::new(ExecBash::new())?)` (or `.add_tool(Arc::new(ExecBash::new()))` if `add_tool` is infallible — match the existing AddTool pattern).
2. Replace the ARCHITECTURE.md `### ExecBash` placeholder stub with a real paragraph documenting: input shape, output shape, error variants, and edge cases.

## Required changes

### 1. `src/lib.rs`

Find the existing line that registers AddTool (likely inside `run_async` or `default_harness_builder` — search for `AddTool::new` or `add_tool(Arc::new`). Add a corresponding line for `ExecBash::new()`. Use the same Arc/error-handling pattern as AddTool. The line should be alphabetically or logically adjacent (group built-ins together).

If `crate::builtins::ExecBash` is not yet importable at the registration site, add the import.

### 2. `ARCHITECTURE.md`

Replace the line `*Stub -- filled in by the ExecBash registration bead.*` under `### ExecBash` with a real paragraph. Suggested content (rephrase/restructure as you see fit; keep it concise and match the existing prose voice):

```markdown
### ExecBash

Executes a single bash command via the `Shell` trait (real impl: `BashShell`,
which spawns `/bin/bash -c <command>`). Input: `{command: String,
timeout_seconds?: u64}`; the default timeout is 30 seconds. Output:
`{stdout, stderr, status, stdout_truncated, stderr_truncated,
has_invalid_utf8_stdout, has_invalid_utf8_stderr, execution_id}`. The
returned stdout/stderr are the **last 64 KiB** of each stream
(tail-truncated), converted via `String::from_utf8_lossy`; the
`has_invalid_utf8_*` flags are `true` iff lossy conversion replaced any
bytes. `status` is one of `{status:"exit", code}`, `{status:"signal",
name}`, or `{status:"timeout"}`. `execution_id` is a hyphenless UUID v4
(32 hex chars) that names the full-output tmp files staged at
`tempdir()/pristine-{pid}/{execution_id}.{stdout,stderr}` (best-effort
write; failures are logged but do not fail the call). Error variants
(serialized as `ToolError::Execution(Value)`): `Spawn { reason }`, `Io {
reason }`, `TmpFile { reason }`. Malformed input (missing `command`,
non-string, etc.) surfaces as the engine-level `ToolError::InvalidInput`,
not an ExecBash-dialect error.
```

If the existing ExecBash stub has subtle nuance (e.g., specific wording or formatting), match it. Do NOT touch the other tool stubs.

## Out of scope

- Changes to other tools' stubs or registrations.
- Changes to SYSTEM_PROMPT (that's an EOC bead).
- Tests of any kind (those are in T-3, already done).
- DESIGN.md updates (EOC bead).

## Definition of Done

- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo nextest run` -- count unchanged at 104 passed, 6 skipped
- `jj diff --summary` shows exactly `M src/lib.rs` and `M ARCHITECTURE.md`. No other files.
- Commit subject `Close bd-XXX: ExecBash T-4 (registration)` (or similar) WITH the trailer.

## Plan-respect note

Registration is dialect-driven: each tool's registration call is a one-liner that the harness simply runs. The `add_tool` API is the portable interface; what each tool DOES is the dialect.

title: Built-in tools P-4: ARCHITECTURE doc-prep
description:

   ### Read

   ### Edit

   *Stub -- filled in by the Edit registration bead.*

   ### Insert
